daa 0.99.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Introduction to daa

Source: vignettes/daa.Rmd
daa.Rmd

Introduction

The daa package provides methods for differential abundance analysis (DAA) of microbiome data. It works with TreeSummarizedExperiment and SummarizedExperiment objects from the miaverse ecosystem.

Installation 

if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("BiocManager")
}

BiocManager::install("daa")

Available Methods

Wilcoxon rank-sum test

The getWilcoxonTest() function performs nonparametric Wilcoxon rank-sum tests for each feature, comparing two groups.

library(daa)
library(mia)

# Load example data
data(peerj13075, package = "mia")
tse <- peerj13075

# Agglomerate to phylum level
tse <- agglomerateByRank(tse, "phylum")

# Run Wilcoxon test
res <- getWilcoxonTest(tse, counts ~ Geographical_location)
head(res)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 10
#>   rownames    .y.   group1 group2    n1    n2 statistic     p p.adj p.adj.signif
#>   <chr>       <chr> <chr>  <chr>  <int> <int>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>       
#> 1 Acidobacte… coun… Ahmed… Pune      11    36      210. 0.834 0.834 ns          
#> 2 Actinobact… coun… Ahmed… Nashik    11    11       78  0.27  0.54  ns          
#> 3 Actinobact… coun… Ahmed… Pune      11    36      178. 0.633 0.633 ns          
#> 4 Actinobact… coun… Nashik Pune      11    36      126  0.072 0.216 ns          
#> 5 Bacteroide… coun… Ahmed… Nashik    11    11       57  0.832 0.832 ns          
#> 6 Bacteroide… coun… Ahmed… Pune      11    36      279  0.035 0.071 ns

t-test

The getTTest() function performs t-tests (Welch’s by default).

res <- getTTest(tse, counts ~ Geographical_location)
head(res)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 11
#>   rownames       .y.    group1    group2    n1    n2 statistic    df     p p.adj
#>   <chr>          <chr>  <chr>     <chr>  <int> <int>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
#> 1 Acidobacteria  counts Ahmednag… Pune      11    36     0.664  11.9 0.519 0.519
#> 2 Actinobacteria counts Ahmednag… Nashik    11    11     1.01   11.7 0.334 0.668
#> 3 Actinobacteria counts Ahmednag… Pune      11    36    -0.177  16.9 0.862 0.862
#> 4 Actinobacteria counts Nashik    Pune      11    36    -1.97   45.0 0.056 0.166
#> 5 Bacteroidetes  counts Ahmednag… Nashik    11    11    -0.749  10.6 0.47  0.514
#> 6 Bacteroidetes  counts Ahmednag… Pune      11    36     2.44   10.5 0.034 0.101
#> # ℹ 1 more variable: p.adj.signif <chr>

ANOVA and Kruskal-Wallis

For comparing more than two groups:

res <- getAnova(tse, counts ~ Geographical_location)
head(res)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 8
#>   rownames                   Effect          DFn   DFd     F     p `p<.05`   ges
#>   <chr>                      <chr>         <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>   <dbl>
#> 1 Acidobacteria              Geographical…     1    45 0.803 0.375 ""      0.018
#> 2 Actinobacteria             Geographical…     2    55 0.7   0.501 ""      0.025
#> 3 Bacteroidetes              Geographical…     2    55 2.99  0.059 ""      0.098
#> 4 Caldiserica                Geographical…     2    55 1.03  0.364 ""      0.036
#> 5 Candidatus Melainabacteria Geographical…     2    55 0.536 0.588 ""      0.019
#> 6 Chloroflexi                Geographical…     2    55 5.41  0.007 "*"     0.164
res <- getKruskal(tse, counts ~ Geographical_location)
head(res)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 7
#>   rownames                   .y.        n statistic    df       p method        
#>   <chr>                      <chr>  <int>     <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>         
#> 1 Acidobacteria              counts    47     0.807     1 0.369   Kruskal-Wallis
#> 2 Actinobacteria             counts    58     3.37      2 0.185   Kruskal-Wallis
#> 3 Bacteroidetes              counts    58     8.91      2 0.0116  Kruskal-Wallis
#> 4 Caldiserica                counts    58     1.14      2 0.567   Kruskal-Wallis
#> 5 Candidatus Melainabacteria counts    58     1.09      2 0.58    Kruskal-Wallis
#> 6 Chloroflexi                counts    58     9.67      2 0.00796 Kruskal-Wallis

Testing metadata variables

You can also test variables from colData. For example, testing alpha diversity indices:

# Add alpha diversity to colData
tse <- mia::addAlpha(tse, index = "shannon")

# Test diversity differences between groups
res <- getWilcoxonTest(tse, shannon ~ Geographical_location)
head(res)
#> # A tibble: 3 × 10
#>   rownames .y.     group1  group2    n1    n2 statistic     p p.adj p.adj.signif
#>   <chr>    <chr>   <chr>   <chr>  <int> <int>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>       
#> 1 shannon  shannon Ahmedn… Nashik    11    11        85 0.116 0.232 ns          
#> 2 shannon  shannon Ahmedn… Pune      11    36       181 0.683 0.683 ns          
#> 3 shannon  shannon Nashik  Pune      11    36       120 0.051 0.152 ns

Wide-format methods

For model-based approaches, use formula with the assay name on the LHS:

# Linear model per feature
tse <- transformAssay(tse, method = "rclr")
res <- getLm(tse, rclr ~ Geographical_location)

# Limma
fit <- getLimma(tse, counts ~ Geographical_location)

Session Info 

sessionInfo()
#> R version 4.6.0 (2026-04-24)
#> Platform: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu
#> Running under: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS
#> 
#> Matrix products: default
#> BLAS:   /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/openblas-pthread/libblas.so.3 
#> LAPACK: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/openblas-pthread/libopenblasp-r0.3.26.so;  LAPACK version 3.12.0
#> 
#> locale:
#>  [1] LC_CTYPE=en_US.UTF-8       LC_NUMERIC=C              
#>  [3] LC_TIME=en_US.UTF-8        LC_COLLATE=en_US.UTF-8

#> [1] stats4    stats     graphics  grDevices utils     datasets  methods

#>  [1] mia_1.20.0                      TreeSummarizedExperiment_2.20.0
